body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7811/24c69fd9/eb31deb9/18e553e8/2474880183/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact&source=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact")
---

Peter,

Run to Win is hitting the road!

Since Election Day, we've heard from over 12,000 women who are interested in running for office and nearly 7,000 people who want to help them run.

To help these women, we're excited to announce our most extensive training schedule EVER for women thinking about running for office.

**[Check out the schedule below, then sign up to get more information as soon as details are confirmed.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7811/24c69fd9/eb31deb9/18e553e9/2474880183/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20170503_website_TrainingInterest?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Check+out+the+schedule+below+then+sign+u&utm_campaign=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact&source=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact")**
**RUN TO WIN 2017 TRAINING SCHEDULE**

**Spring 2017**
Michigan
Chicago, Illinois
Florida
Seattle, Washington

**Summer 2017**
Virginia (for current 2017 candidates)
Pennsylvania
Colorado
Michigan
New Mexico
Nevada
Los Angeles, California
Minnesota
Georgia

**Fall 2017**
New York, New York
San Francisco, California
Dallas, Texas
Maine
North Carolina
New Hampshire
Ohio
Washington, D.C.
If you don't see a location near you on the list, don't worry! We'll be adding more in the months to come and rolling out resources online too -- **[you can sign up here if you'd like to receive online resources and webinars.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7811/24c69fd9/eb31deb9/18e553ea/2474880183/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20170503_website_TrainingInterest?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+you+can+sign+up+here+if+youd+like+to+rec&utm_campaign=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact&source=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact")**

Since November, EMILY's List has seen an outpouring of interest from women who want to learn how to run for office. As an organization whose mission is to elect as many pro-choice Democratic women as possible, we set out to meet the enthusiasm we were seeing with a scaled up program to match.

Run to Win candidate trainings are a great way to learn the nuts and bolts of running for office, whether you're thinking about running or already in a race.

These half-day trainings cover all the basics, including questions to ask yourself, things to start thinking about, and the concrete steps to get there. They're also a great way to meet other women in your community who are considering a run for office too.

**Sign up to stay updated on trainings near you:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Trainings-Information](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7811/24c69fd9/eb31deb9/18e553eb/2474880183/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20170503_website_TrainingInterest?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgTrainingsInforma&utm_campaign=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact&source=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact")**

Thank you,

Mũthoni Wambu Kraal
Vice President of National Outreach and Training, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7811/24c69fd9/eb31deb9/18e553e4/2474880183/VEsD/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=5&utm_campaign=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact&source=WQENE517N_digital_email_20170511_AnnounceTrainings_inact")

name: Tom Perez
subject: re: the new CEO of the Democratic Party (sign the card)
body:
I'm excited to introduce you to Jess O'Connell
---
**Sign our card to welcome the Democratic Party's new CEO, Jess O'Connell.

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Welcome-Jess-OConnell1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1")**
Friend --

>From my very first day as Chair, we've been on a mission to rebuild and reshape the Democratic Party from the grassroots up. We need to organize in every state and every zip code if we're going to win -- not just at election time, but all year round.

Grassroots supporters like you power our party, and because you're so critical to our success, I wanted to make sure you were one of the first to know some big news.

**[Today, I'm excited to announce that Jess O'Connell will be the next CEO of the Democratic Party. Join me in welcoming her to our team.](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Welcome-Jess-OConnell1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Today+Im+excited+to+announce+that+Jess+O&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1")**

As I've traveled and met with Democrats like you across the country, one of the things I've heard most is that our party needs to get back to basics -- getting active in local communities and talking about our values and vision for the future of our nation.

There's no one better than Jess O'Connell to help us do it.

Jess is coming to us from EMILY's List, where she's led that organization to historic success and helped Democrats achieve some of our brightest electoral victories over the past four years.

Jess has helped elect the most diverse Congress in history -- including quadrupling the number of women of color in the Senate -- and has built programs to elect hundreds of Democratic women to state and local offices around the country.

I know that Jess can help us fulfill the vision that all of us have for the Democratic Party because she's a grassroots organizer at her core. And I'm confident that she can communicate our party's message of opportunity and inclusion to voters in every corner of every state because she's lived it, both through her lifelong work to advance progressive policies that help working families and because she'll be stepping into her new role as the first openly LGBT person to run the Democratic Party.

We have some immensely important work ahead of us. We've all witnessed the chaos and harm that Donald Trump and the GOP have inflicted on working families.

It's on all of us to turn our party around and get back on track so we can win elections this year, in 2018, and beyond. With Jess at the helm, I know we will.

**Sign our card today to welcome Jess, and let her know we're ready to get to work:**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Welcome-Jess-OConnell1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1")**
Thanks -- now let's get to it. We've got a lot to do, but I know there's a bright future ahead for Democrats.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052cffe/4067b0a/2faac9790/7e77e46d/1626124701/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052cffe/4067b0a/2faac9790/7e77e46a/1626124701/VEsD/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052cffe/4067b0a/2faac9790/7e77e46b/1626124701/VEsA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052cffe/4067b0a/2faac9790/7e77e468/1626124701/VEsB/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170512_tp_nd1&source=em_20170512_tp_nd1"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Bob =?UTF-8?Q?Fertik=2C=20Democrats=2Ecom?=
subject: POLITICO: McConnell rejects call for special prosecutor
body:
Activist -- These are the facts:

This week, Donald Trump fired FBI Director James Comey in the midst of an ongoing investigation into whether Russia interfered to aid Trump's campaign in the 2016 election.

And Attorney General Jeff Sessions, who recused himself from the FBI investigation after lying about his own meetings with the Russians, played a major role in recommending Comey's removal.

Then, Mitch McConnell defended Trump's decision and dismissed calls for further investigation by calling it "partisan" and that it would "impede the current work being done."

This is outrageous. The American people deserve the truth on what's happened -- and the only way to find it out is with an independent investigation headed by a special prosecutor.

Add your name right now to call for a special prosecutor to investigate Russian interference in the 2016 election and the Trump administration's dealings with Russia (https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/demand-special-prosecutor?source=SEM_demsdotcom_051217_SpecPros_ng) .

Congress must act immediately to restore accountability into the Russia investigation after this attack on the FBI, no matter Republicans' attempts to sweep this under the rug.

A special prosecutor is absolutely necessary now to carry out an independent investigation -- one who is bolstered by Congressional oversight and cannot be supervised by Jeff Sessions or the White House.

We won't know what could be covered up until we get a special prosecutor to discover the full extent of Trump's Russia connections. Add your name to stand with us (https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/demand-special-prosecutor?source=SEM_demsdotcom_051217_SpecPros_ng) .

Thanks for taking a stand,

Team DSCC

Democrats.com, Box 1452, New York, NY 10276

body:
We need a special prosecutor to investigate Trump's ties to Russia.
---
**Add your name to demand an independent investigation into Trump and his connections to Russia.

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Investigate-Trump-and-Russia1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170512_dnc_nd&source=em_20170512_dnc_nd")**

Friend --

Each new piece of information that comes to light about Donald Trump's firing of James Comey gets more and more disturbing.

We now know that the FBI's investigation into Trump's ties to Russia was heating up when Trump made his decision -- Comey had just requested more resources and manpower.

In an interview with NBC News' Lester Holt last night, Donald Trump openly admitted that the escalating Russia investigation was part of his rationale for firing Comey.

And just this morning, Donald Trump publicly threatened the former FBI director, tweeting that he would release tapes of his conversations with Comey if Comey were to reveal what he knows.

Donald Trump is actively interfering with the investigation into himself, his campaign, and Russian interference in our election. And Republicans in Congress are letting him get away with it.

Paul Ryan and Mitch McConnell have both suggested there's nothing to see here and that there's no need for an independent investigation into Trump and Russia. Hundreds of thousands of Democrats have already signed on to tell them they're wrong.

**Join them today -- sign your name to demand an independent investigation led by a special prosecutor. Stop this corrupt administration and complicit Congress:**

body:
---
Derek -- The health care bill passed by House Republicans last week is incredibly disappointing. Despite promises from President Trump that no one would lose coverage under a Republican health care bill, this bill would:
* Cause at least 24 million Americans to lose health insurance

* Result in increases to the cost of care, especially for seniors

* Make health care unaffordable for many of the _130 million_ Americans with pre-existing conditions

**Republicans seem more interested in giving their wealthy donors a tax break than in actually providing Americans with affordable health care. We need to show them they won 't get away with it.**

Tim is going to fight this bill in the Senate as hard as he can. But he needs your support. **[Will you chip in right away to show Tim you support his efforts to save health care for Virginians?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=j12POPROYzqrl4nPvtnBhIgq%2F%2BA96gYy)**

Republicans in the House were so anxious to pass this bill that they didn't fully consider how it would affect millions of Americans. The Senate can't make the same mistake. **As Senator, and especially as a member of the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions, Tim will fight to make sure that no bill is passed that results in Virginians losing their care.**

This won't be an easy fight. Stopping them is going to take a massive grassroots effort.

But with leaders like Tim, we can get it done. **[Will you pitch in to help Tim stay in the Senate and keep fighting for our health care?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=jVxH%2BvFcaqW67Fa8wT72Mogq%2F%2BA96gYy)** Your support will show Republicans that we're not backing down -- we're only getting more energized.

Thanks,
Team Kaine

| | Peter,

Last week, House Republicans passed their "American Health Care Act," which would repeal key provisions of the Affordable Care Act and endanger health care for millions of Americans.

One of the most concerning provisions in the bill is that it would allow insurance companies to go back to doing what they did before passage of the ACA in 2009 -- discriminate against people because of their pre-existing conditions.

Health experts estimate that one in four Americans has a pre-existing condition. **In Indiana, that 's over 1.6 million people who could be affected.** And that's to say nothing about the other, equally alarming aspects of this bill -- like the fact that it would kick _hundreds of thousands_ of Hoosiers off of HIP 2.0.

**Joe wants to hear from you:[Will you take his survey about how the Republicans' American Health Care Act might affect you?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c267d/7377f838/36040436/51fb4ee6/601790551/VEsE/ "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/M7NL3NB")**

**_Live from San Diego_**
Hello from the Republican National Committee Spring Meeting in San Diego, California. I'm here this week with our National Committeeman **John Hammond** (more about him below) and Executive Director of Strategic Communications & Talent Development **Pete Seat** to meet with fellow RNC members as we learn about the latest tools we have at our disposal to elect proven Republican leaders up and down the ticket. It's been energizing to hear about the successes my counterparts across the country have had recently, but to be honest, nothing can compare to the success we've had in Indiana.

In fact, I've had some fun bragging on Indiana being the home of Vice President Mike Pence and the success we had just this past Monday when 1,000 Hoosier Republicans came together to hear from Donald Trump, Jr. at our 2017 Indiana Republican Party Spring Dinner. Although, to say the event was a success is an understatement. It was the highest grossing Indiana GOP fundraising event in memory and assembled the second largest State Dinner crowd we've had in well over a generation.

A huge thanks goes out to Governor **Eric Holcomb** , Lt. Governor **Suzanne Crouch** , Speaker **Brian Bosma** , Senator **David Long** , Secretary of State **Connie Lawson** , Treasurer **Kelly Mitchell** , Auditor **Tera Klutz** , Congressman **Luke Messer** and Congressman **Todd Rokita** for helping to sponsor the event and to all the **county party organizations** that bought tables. If that's what we can pull off in a non-election year, just imagine what 2018 will bring for Indiana's Party of Purpose!

\- Kyle

(Photo: Governor Eric Holcomb and Chairman Hupfer greet each other on stage at the 2017 Indiana Republican Party Spring Dinner at the JW Marriott in Indianapolis on Monday, May 8, 2017.)

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: This is for you
body:
#
Team --
Earlier this week, I introduced the guidelines of a bill to stop insurance companies from charging more or denying benefits to Floridians based on preexisting conditions or their gender.
[**You can become a citizen co-sponsor of this bill by adding your name right here, right now >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3345/9e00690/48420690/24037545/709009587/VEsH/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/co-sponsor?source=em170512-full")
Here are a few stories from Floridians like you, who inspired me to propose these important protections:
> _" My 40 year old daughter was diagnosed with stage 2 breast cancer last year. Thank God she has Obamacare with good coverage and a subsidy that makes her insurance affordable. She is currently in treatment at Moffitt Cancer Center in Tampa. I went to a town hall meeting for Congressman Webster, shook his hand and told him this story. I asked him if his vote on healthcare would continue to give my daughter affordable coverage. He said "Yes." I then asked him if he would give me his word on his vote . He said "Yes." He lied."_
> \- Carol, Lake County
> _" I have diabetes and heart problems and prior to ACA my premiums were $1200 per month. Now I pay $400 per month through the exchange and have a good policy with low deductibles. Insurance companies make billions in profit and pharmaceuticals charge outrageous fees for a drug I need to stay alive. If I can not afford insulin I will die."_
> \- Judy, Pinellas County
> _" I am currently battling ovarian cancer and am in a fight for my life. Without the subsidy we receive on Obamacare we could not afford health insurance."_
> \- Carlyn, Hillsborough County
[**If you agree that we need to protect our fellow Floridians from losing their health care they need, add your name now >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3345/9e00690/48420690/24037545/709009587/VEsE/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/co-sponsor?source=em170512-full")
We won't stop fighting for you.
Bring it home,
Andrew

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3345/9e00690/48420690/24037544/709009587/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3345/9e00690/48420690/24037547/709009587/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3345/9e00690/48420690/24037546/709009587/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, May 12th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
“The State of our party is strong!,” said Republican National Committee Co-Chair Bob Paduchik this morning.
Exciting times for Republicans!

This past week National Committeeman Steve Duprey, National Committeewoman Juliana Bergeron, and I attended the Spring Meeting of the RNC. The enthusiasm was amazing and the agenda to grow our majorities is a serious one.
RNC Chair Ronna McDaniel advised that fundraising has been the strongest it has been in years – far surpassing the Democrats efforts. I believe this is a testament that the American voter BELIEVES Republican ideas and philosophy is the right path to make America First Again.
The White House is committed to helping NH grow our majorities. For the second time in a two week period, Kellyanne Conway, Counselor to the President, will be back in New Hampshire – the second time to help raise money for New Hampshire Republicans!
I hope you will consider investing in our future by [_**attending the event on Thursday, May 18th**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbVCInQgtZVu409EhFI-2BClEJcu2CUmW89Ku819D2SduyT_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MqY1rh615WSSkD5QkMb1kcleaBi2JSo8boocVI1XJe7bhNBLBy0YtD7UaNVUnNXq0LUItyVRmlU-2BCphL8-2FiuZtHqtrUopKHmfB40KJB6kqSVgy6MWI6YTuNL8TO0zfDZJqAQk5AJykI-2Bp0Ocrkpl1g7yaUPh0whFydW-2Br52g1ivOOOsqYk9Ev2-2FM1eB9Xm6ozeSyj3f49rGcHIq91-2F2zD3K4WOVR80iswHN19KjlIvnssplueO3tOoJf6O5-2FaZt-2FY5kyx-2BiG3j4CFpHAWR9RF5g-3D-3D). If you can’t attend, no worries. There are always opportunities to show your support—join a local committee, volunteer in one of the upcoming special elections, write a letter to the editor about why Republicans have the best ideas and will make this state and country great again. And last, but not least, please consider joining our [_**Grassroots Circle program**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurnZ5zJfDwV5-2F8Hl4xre-2BIMt5nd0pHim8EqEA9pV9ZCkm_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MqY1rh615WSSkD5QkMb1kcleaBi2JSo8boocVI1XJe7bhNBLBy0YtD7UaNVUnNXq0LUItyVRmlU-2BCphL8-2FiuZtMauRxt2zADMHK-2FL8c6RPx4O3kf0lah-2Fc1hQ5wkWpEQdwsVoxDXmhEmxksAIgOCGWaJU-2F0Ik9Di6HincMnW6ueBdkKUNYjLiyUTGByc935H3tTauLvDDfjMKhCw-2BlTn36Qi8OzOUqK3x95fx7JknC2VOnMWl3LkillnGk6bP996zil7RTm8MJ6g26waFY-2FIoYA-3D-3D).

I hope to see you all this coming Thursday!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

**NH National Guard Facility Gets New Name**
On Tuesday Governor Chris Sununu flanked by Deputy Speaker Gene Chandler, Adjutant General Reddel, and other members of the NH Guard signed HB 508 into law. HB 508 sponsored by Rep. Gene Chandler names the New Hampshire Army National Guard Regional Training Institute and Barracks facility in Pembroke after Civil War hero Colonel Edward Cross. Col. Cross was tragically killed during the Battle of Gettysburg when he was shot by a Confederate sniper. His portrait along with his presentation sword can be found on the first floor of the State House. The NH National Guard’s Edward Cross Training Complex will now serve as a fitting memorial to one of our states Civil War heroes. | _**Senate Republicans**_
**Senators tout full support of expanded mental health care, DCYF restructuring**
Concord, NH – Today, the Senate passed a bill, HB 400, which would provide additional resources for mental health care while making key reforms to the Division of Children Youth and Families to ensure New Hampshire laws prioritize child safety.
Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem), Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro), and Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua) issued the following statements:
“After visiting a local hospital and seeing the number of people with mental illness who are waiting for a bed at New Hampshire Hospital and receiving twice-daily reports showing a staggering number of people waiting from across the state, we took immediate action to develop a plan that would expand access to critical mental health services,” Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem).
“This legislation is designed to quickly increase community mental health services by adding 68 treatment beds including additional beds at receiving facilities, transitional and community residential services, and peer respite beds. We wanted to address this issue from all sides and including additional mobile crisis teams helps increase access to treatment that those with mental illness need,” said Morse.
“New Hampshire is facing a mental health crisis as people who are our friends and neighbors wait for days and sometimes weeks for treatment of their disease. Passage of HB 400 represents a significant step towards mitigating this unacceptable situation,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro).
“I am pleased to support increasing mental health services in this legislation. After hearing compelling testimony from citizens with mental health conditions, it is increasingly clear that our state needs to do more and offer expanded care services in our communities,” said Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), vice-chair of the Senate Health and Human Services Committee. “This legislation is a positive step forward and we will continue to work hard to provide effective care solutions to mental health patients across our state.”
“This legislature also recognizes the need to ensure our state’s children are protected. I am grateful that Senator Sharon Carson first introduced steps to restructure the Division of Children Youth and Families [DCYF] to further ensure children are given protections from neglect and abuse. This bill authorizes the creation of the office of the child advocate to provide greater oversight in the DCYF aimed at improving the safety of our children,” said Bradley.
“These two issues are critically important to our state and we are pleased that this bill received the Senate’s support today and we look forward to Governor Sununu signing these measures into law,” said Bradley. |

The enthusiasm was amazing and the agenda to grow our majorities is a serious one. RNC Chair Ronna McDaniel advised that fundraising has been the strongest it has been in years – far surpassing the Democrats efforts. I believe this is a testament that the American voter BELIEVES Republican ideas and philosophy is the right path to make America First Again. The White House is committed to helping NH grow our majorities. For the second time in a two week period, Kellyanne Conway, Counselor to the President, will be back in New Hampshire – the second time to help raise money for New Hampshire Republicans! I hope you will consider investing in our future by . If you can’t attend, no worries. HB 508 sponsored by Rep. Gene Chandler names the New Hampshire Army National Guard Regional Training Institute and Barracks facility in Pembroke after Civil War hero Colonel Edward Cross. Col. Cross was tragically killed during the Battle of Gettysburg when he was shot by a Confederate sniper. His portrait along with his presentation sword can be found on the first floor of the State House. The NH National Guard’s Edward Cross Training Complex will now serve as a fitting memorial to one of our states Civil War heroes. If you’d like to help Mark McLean in the Hillsborough 44 race please reach out to NHGOP Political Director Tara Sennick atmailto:tara@nhgop.org?subject=I%20would%20like%20to%20help%20with%20the%20Special%20Election!. The special elections for these House races will both be held on Tuesday, May 23rd. | | Senate Republicans Senators tout full support of expanded mental health care, DCYF restructuring Concord, NH – Today, the Senate passed a bill, HB 400, which would provide additional resources for mental health care while making key reforms to the Division of Children Youth and Families to ensure New Hampshire laws prioritize child safety. Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem), Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro), and Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua) issued the following statements: “After visiting a local hospital and seeing the number of people with mental illness who are waiting for a bed at New Hampshire Hospital and receiving twice-daily reports showing a staggering number of people waiting from across the state, we took immediate action to develop a plan that would expand access to critical mental health services,” Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This legislation is designed to quickly increase community mental health services by adding 68 treatment beds including additional beds at receiving facilities, transitional and community residential services, and peer respite beds. We wanted to address this issue from all sides and including additional mobile crisis teams helps increase access to treatment that those with mental illness need,” said Morse. “New Hampshire is facing a mental health crisis as people who are our friends and neighbors wait for days and sometimes weeks for treatment of their disease. Passage of HB 400 represents a significant step towards mitigating this unacceptable situation,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro). “I am pleased to support increasing mental health services in this legislation. After hearing compelling testimony from citizens with mental health conditions, it is increasingly clear that our state needs to do more and offer expanded care services in our communities,” said Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), vice-chair of the Senate Health and Human Services Committee. “This legislation is a positive step forward and we will continue to work hard to provide effective care solutions to mental health patients across our state.” “This legislature also recognizes the need to ensure our state’s children are protected. I am grateful that Senator Sharon Carson first introduced steps to restructure the Division of Children Youth and Families [DCYF] to further ensure children are given protections from neglect and abuse. This bill authorizes the creation of the office of the child advocate to provide greater oversight in the DCYF aimed at improving the safety of our children,” said Bradley. “These two issues are critically important to our state and we are pleased that this bill received the Senate’s support today and we look forward to Governor Sununu signing these measures into law,” said Bradley. | | NHGOP Press Releases | ---|---|--- | May 10 | | News from Washington... | ---|---|--- | This week, President Trump asked NH Secretary of State, Bill Gardner, to serve on a commission tasked with examining federal election law, potential for voter fraud, and steps to improve confidence in voting procedures. | | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director -** | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--
